title: Multimodal Fusion Architectures
summary: Combining information from different modalities (text, image, audio, graph). Three strategies: early fusion (concatenate inputs), late fusion (combine outputs), cross-attention fusion (one modality queries another). Cross-attention is the dominant approach in modern systems.
key_insights: Early fusion: concatenate modality features before processing. Simple but forces same architecture for all modalities.
Late fusion: process each modality independently, combine at the end. Easy to build but no cross-modal interaction during processing.
Cross-attention fusion: one modality provides Q, the other provides K/V. Rich interaction. Used in Stable Diffusion (text→image), Flamingo (image→text).
Dual-encoder pattern: separate encoders per modality, align in shared embedding space. Used in CLIP (image-text matching).
Contrastive pre-training (CLIP): maximize similarity of matching image-text pairs, minimize for non-matching. Creates aligned multimodal space.
OQ1 connects here: should residual bypass cross-attention in dual-encoder? Bypass = refine modality representation. No bypass = full replacement with cross-modal info.

mathematical_core.early_fusion: $z = f([x_{\text{text}} ; x_{\text{image}}])$ — concatenate, then shared encoder
mathematical_core.late_fusion: $z = g(f_{\text{text}}(x_{\text{text}}), f_{\text{image}}(x_{\text{image}}))$ — separate encoders, combine predictions
mathematical_core.clip_loss: $$L = -\frac{1}{2N}\sum_i \left[\log \frac{e^{\text{sim}(t_i, v_i)/\tau}}{\sum_j e^{\text{sim}(t_i, v_j)/\tau}} + \log \frac{e^{\text{sim}(v_i, t_i)/\tau}}{\sum_j e^{\text{sim}(v_i, t_j)/\tau}}\right]$$

derivations.title: Cross-attention score matrix is rectangular — key difference from self-attention

steps.name: Self-attention
steps.math: Score matrix $\in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$. Square. Each position attends to all others in same modality.

steps.name: Cross-attention
steps.math: Score matrix $\in \mathbb{R}^{n_{\text{text}} \times n_{\text{image}}}$. Rectangular. Text tokens query image patches.

steps.name: Asymmetry
steps.math: Text word 'dog' can attend to image patch containing dog. But image patch doesn't directly query text. Direction matters — who provides Q controls the output shape.

derivations.consequence: Cross-attention output has the shape/length of the Q source. Text-to-image cross-attention outputs text-length sequence enriched with visual info.

open_questions: OQ1: Should residual bypass cross-attention in dual-encoder? Bypass = refinement, no bypass = replacement.
notes: Stable Diffusion = VAE (compress) + diffusion (generate in latent space) + cross-attention (text conditions image generation). The cross-attention from TRANSFORMER.CROSS_ATTENTION is the exact mechanism.
